title: Add Excel Import/Export to Inventory Page

requirements.text: Add an Excel export (download) button to the Inventory page that exports all inventory items to a well-formatted .xlsx file. The exported file must include columns: Name, Type (Goods/Service), Purchase Price, Selling Price, Stock, and Unit. The file must be compatible with all Excel versions and must NOT use CSV format.

quotes: bisa ditambahkan menu unggah dan unduh exle dengan format axle (bukan csv) yang rapi bisa compatible dengan semua versi excel

acceptanceCriteria: A clearly labeled 'Download Excel' button is visible on the Inventory page toolbar.
Clicking the button triggers a download of a .xlsx file (not .csv).
The downloaded file contains all current inventory items with columns: Name, Type, Purchase Price, Selling Price, Stock, Unit.
The file opens correctly in Excel 2007 and newer versions.
Column headers are bold and clearly formatted.
Numeric price and stock values are stored as numbers (not strings) in the spreadsheet.

requirements.text: Add an Excel import (upload) button to the Inventory page that allows users to upload a .xlsx file to bulk-import inventory items. The import logic must correctly parse both semicolon (;) and comma (,) column separators to ensure compatibility across all regional Excel versions. After a successful import, the inventory list must refresh to show the newly imported items.

quotes: bisa ditambahkan menu unggah dan unduh exle dengan format axle (bukan csv) yang rapi bisa compatible dengan semua versi excel baik yang menggunakan tanda pemisah colom ; atau ,

acceptanceCriteria: A clearly labeled 'Upload Excel' button is visible on the Inventory page toolbar.
Clicking the button opens a file picker that accepts .xlsx files.
The system correctly parses .xlsx files regardless of whether the regional Excel setting uses ; or , as separators.
Imported rows are validated: Name is required; Type must be 'goods' or 'service'; numeric fields (prices, stock) must be valid numbers.
Invalid rows are reported to the user with a clear error message indicating which rows failed and why.
Successfully imported items are added to the backend inventory and the inventory table refreshes automatically.
Duplicate items (by name) prompt the user to choose to skip or overwrite.

requirements.text: Create a downloadable Excel template file that users can download before importing, pre-filled with the correct column headers (Name, Type, Purchase Price, Selling Price, Stock, Unit) and one example row. This helps users prepare their data in the correct format.

quotes: menu unggah dan unduh exle dengan format axle (bukan csv) yang rapi

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Download Template' link or button is available near the Upload Excel button.
The template .xlsx file contains bold headers: Name, Type, Purchase Price, Selling Price, Stock, Unit.
One example row is included demonstrating the expected format (e.g., 'Brake Pad, goods, 50000, 75000, 10, pcs').
The template file opens correctly in all Excel versions.

constraints: Export and import must use .xlsx format — CSV format is explicitly excluded.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Excel parsing must handle both semicolon (;) and comma (,) column delimiters for regional Excel compatibility.
All user-facing text must be in English.
nonGoals: Exporting or importing transaction history or reports.
Cloud storage or server-side file storage of uploaded Excel files.
Real-time collaboration or multi-user import conflict resolution.
